title: Component
description: A finer-grained part inside a module, such as an attention head or projection layer.
problemStatement: Engineers talk about heads, projections, and norms as "layers," but the atlas needs a consistent name for pieces smaller than a full module.
coreIdea: A component is a sub-part of a module that still does one identifiable job in the forward pass.

whatItIs.title: What It Is
whatItIs.body: Components include query/key/value projections, individual attention heads, layer-normalization cells, and gating units inside a larger block. They are the smallest units this taxonomy names before dropping to raw tensor ops.

whyItMatters.title: Why It Matters
whyItMatters.body: Clear component language helps you read architecture diagrams, follow sharding plans, and understand which part of a module changes when papers propose a new projection or norm placement.

simpleExample.title: Simple Example
simpleExample.body: Multi-head attention is a module; each head is a component with its own Q/K/V projections and output merge. Grouped-query attention changes how those components share key/value state.

whereItAppears.title: Where It Appears
whereItAppears.body: Component labels appear in detailed module diagrams, efficient-attention write-ups, and hardware partitioning docs. Module pages in this atlas may reference components when explaining internals.

commonConfusions.title: Common Confusions
commonConfusions.body: Components are not standalone modules—you rarely swap only a norm and call it a new architecture. They are also not parameters: a component is a structural role, while weights are the learned values filling that role.

readerShortcut.title: Reader Shortcut
readerShortcut.body: Zoom out one level to "module" when the block has its own page in `/docs/modules/`; zoom in to "component" when you are inside that block.
